description: Secure your code by bundling data with methods and hiding complex implementation details.

slides.title: The Capsule 💊
slides.content:
# Encapsulation

Encapsulation is the process of bundling **data** (variables) and **methods** (functions) that manipulate the data into a single unit (Class).

It also restricts direct access to some of an object's components, which is a means of **preventing accidental interference**.

slides.title: Information Hiding
Getters and Setters
Abstraction
slides.content:
# Hiding Complexity 🎭

**Abstraction** means showing only the essential features of an object to the user and hiding the inner details.

## Analogy: Car
- **Interface (Visible):** Steering wheel, pedals, gear stick.
- **Implementation (Hidden):** Fuel injection, combustion engine, transmission gears.

You don't need to know how the engine works to drive the car!

slides.title: Concept Check
slides.content: Which concept is focused on 'hiding implementation details'?

slides.title: Friend Functions
Full Example: Secure User
Friendship Rules
slides.content: If Class A is a friend of Class B, can Class B automatically access private members of Class A?

slides.title: Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: Security & simplicity 🎓

By bundling and hiding, we create robust software.

## Key Takeaways
- **Encapsulation:** Data + Methods. Protects data using `private`.
- **Getters/Setters:** Controlled access points.
- **Abstraction:** Hiding valid details to reduce complexity.
- **Friend:** Breaking encapsulation rules for trusted entities.

Next, we'll learn about **Templates**, C++'s way of writing generic, reusable code for any data type!
